name: Striped Bass Escabeche with Bell Peppers and Green Beans
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon dried hot red pepper flakes
1/2 cup drained brine-cured green olives, pitted and halved
3 small red bell peppers, cut into very thin rings
3 small yellow bell peppers, cut into very thin rings
1 red onion, cut into very thin rings
6 striped bass fillets with skin, halved lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl combine the marinade ingredients and stir until the brown sugar is dissolved.
2. Add olives, bell peppers, and onion to the marinade. Season fish with salt and pepper and toss with flour, shaking off excess. Place paper towels in a shallow baking dish.
3. Heat 3 tablespoons oil over moderately high heat in a large heavy skillet. Cook fish, 6 pieces at a time, turning once and adding remaining tablespoon oil as needed until golden on both sides and just cooked through, about 6 minutes. Transfer fish to the paper-towel-lined dish to drain.
4. Pour marinade mixture over fish, spreading vegetables in one layer. Marinate fish, covered and chilled, for at least 1 day and up to 3.
5. In a large saucepan of boiling salted water cook beans for 3 minutes or until crisp-tender, then drain in a colander. Refresh beans under cold water and drain. Beans may be cooked 2 days ahead and chilled in a sealable plastic bag.
6. Remove fish from marinade mixture and add beans to the dish, tossing to coat. Transfer mixture to a large deep platter and arrange fish on top.
